destinations.name: Cox's Bazar
destinations.description: Cox's Bazar is home to the world's longest natural sea beach at 120 km. The golden sands, turquoise waters, and seafood scene make it Bangladesh's most-visited destination. Inani Beach and Himchori waterfall are key side trips.

destinations.name: Sajek Valley
destinations.description: Sajek Valley sits at 1800 feet in the Chittagong Hill Tracts and is known as the 'Queen of Hills'. Morning clouds roll through at eye level, creating a sea-of-clouds effect. The valley is home to Ruma and Bagaichari communities.

destinations.name: Sylhet Tea Gardens
destinations.description: Sylhet is famous for endless rolling tea gardens, crystal-clear haor wetlands, and the spiritual site of Hazrat Shah Jalal. Ratargul swamp forest and Jaflong river with its stone collection are must-visits.

destinations.name: Saint Martin's Island
destinations.description: Saint Martin's is Bangladesh's only coral island, a 3 sq km paradise in the Bay of Bengal with crystal-clear water and night sky visibility unmatched anywhere in the country. Accessible only by ship from Teknaf.

destinations.name: Bandarban
destinations.description: Bandarban district hosts Boga Lake, Keokradong, and Bangladesh's highest peak Tajingdong. The Marma, Bawm, and Mro communities give the area a rich cultural depth. Nilachal and Meghla are easy-access viewpoints.

destinations.name: Kuakata
destinations.description: Kuakata is the 'Daughter of the Sea' — one of the few beaches in the world where you can see both sunrise and sunset from the same spot. Rakhain community villages and mangrove forests add cultural depth.
activityList: Sunrise and sunset from the same beach

destinations.name: Rangamati Lake
destinations.description: Rangamati is set around Kaptai Lake, the largest man-made lake in Bangladesh. The floating bridges, hanging bridge over water, and Chakma tribal culture make it uniquely peaceful. The drive along the lake edge is breathtaking.

destinations.name: Sundarbans
destinations.description: The Sundarbans is the world's largest mangrove forest and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Home to the Royal Bengal Tiger, spotted deer, and Irrawaddy dolphins. Only accessible via organised boat tours from Mongla or Khulna.

insights.content: The beach can be dangerously crowded during Eid holidays — prices triple and accommodation runs out. Avoid Eid peak season unless booked 1–2 months ahead.
Jhaubon restaurant near Sugandha Beach is legendary for fresh lobster and crab at local prices. Arrive before 7 PM — sells out fast.
Beach photographers demand 200–500 BDT per photo. Agree on price before they shoot. Also, 'camel' and 'horse' ride operators sometimes demand double after the ride.
Army check post requires National ID. Foreigners need special permit from DC office in Khagrachari. Apply 72 hours before travel.
Cloud sea at Sajek is best between 6–8 AM during October–December. After 9 AM clouds usually lift. Plan first night arrival for best experience.
Try local bamboo chicken (bamboo-cooked rice and chicken) at Ruilui Para — unique CHT dish not found elsewhere. Cost around 150–250 BDT.
Seven-layer tea at Srimangal's Nilkantha Tea Cabin is a must — each coloured layer has a different taste. Costs 60 BDT.
Ratargul Swamp Forest is only accessible by country boat — no life jackets provided. Avoid after heavy rain as currents strengthen.
Ships to Saint Martin stop completely from March–October due to rough seas. Only accessible November–February. This is a hard constraint.
'Island guides' at the dock will insist you need them for BDT 500. The island is tiny and easily walked without a guide. Politely decline.
Chera Dwip beachside stalls serve fresh coconut crab cooked in coconut milk — a delicacy unique to the island. Price: 300–500 BDT per crab.
Boga Lake trek (4–5 hrs one way) requires army permission from Bandarban DC office. Start no later than 7 AM to return before dark.
Nafakhum waterfall is most dramatic June–October when water is highest, but trail gets very slippery. October–November is the ideal balance.
Ilish (Hilsa) fish freshly caught from the Bay of Bengal at Kuakata's beachfront restaurants during June–October season. Best preparation: mustard-cooked ilish.
Since the Padma Bridge opened, Kuakata is now 6–7 hours by bus from Dhaka instead of 12+ hours. Weekend tourism has surged — book hotels ahead.
Chakma tribal restaurants in Rangamati serve Pajon (bamboo curry) and Maita (fermented fish). Located near the tribal museum — easily walkable.
Shuvolong waterfall boat trip adds 4–5 hours. Boat operators sometimes overcharge — the government-set rate is 1200–1500 BDT for a full boat.
Never enter the Sundarbans without an official armed forest guard — tiger encounters are real. All licensed tour operators include this.
Mobile network (Grameenphone best) works only near Mongla and Dublarchar. Deep forest zones have no signal for 2+ days.
Best time to spot Bengal tigers is February–April when they come to river banks for water. Early morning 6–8 AM boat patrols near Kotka.
